name: Timeline View

releases.author: b-camphart
releases.description:
## What's Changed

- #51 :: Visual bug with selecting default properties resolved
- #52 :: Negations should work in filters/groups
- #54 :: Will now display custom properties again


**Full Changelog**: https://github.com/b-camphart/timeline-view/compare/1.4.0...v1.4.1
releases.name: The Sizeable Update 1.4.1

releases.author: b-camphart
releases.description:
# ✨ New Features & Improvements
### 📏 Support for Item Lengths & Resizing
![resize single note](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/fae577b1-7bf1-49ee-b31e-4d1900bce28b)
You can now enable a **secondary property** to define the "length" or "end" value of a note.

- Supports **negative lengths** or **end values** that are less than the primary property.
- Moving notes updates **both properties simultaneously**.
- If a secondary property is enabled, **notes can be resized**.
- Set default secondary properties in the plugin settings.

### 📅 Smarter & More Intuitive Ruler
![ruler dates](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/de5005bd-ec7e-407a-84f6-64fe6ae8ef43)
- when using a date-type property, the ruler will now align with real units of time (hours, days, months, etc.) instead of random increments.
- unlabeled sub-notches have been added to more easily keep track of where you are
- vertical lines now extend through the entire timeline to more easily see how notes align with the values at the top
### 🎨 Design Changes
This release comes with changes to the design to bring it more in line with Obsidian's design.  Namely:
- the selection boundary now matches the design of the selection boundary used in Canvas
- the to make room for the new selection boundary design, notes have been spaced apart
- the horizontal lines in the background of the timeline are subtler
- selected notes no longer hide their group color
### 🎛 Integration with the Style Settings Plugin
Some of the design choices have been made available for customization through the Style Settings plugin.  If you have it installed, you'll be able to customize the spacing, size, and color of the notes.
![style settings](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/5b758dc3-b4bb-487e-be26-9b6a66a28678)
### 🔄 Refined Tab Behavior
To better match Obsidian’s tab system, changes have been made to how the timeline syncs with other tabs:
- The linking option has been removed—no manual linking needed!
- Instead, if another tab gains focus while the timeline is open, the timeline will **automatically focus on the selected note.**

#### 🖌 Better CSS Targeting for Theme Developers
We've made under-the-hood changes to:
- Improve **CSS targetability** for theme developers.
- **Reduce style conflicts** with other plugins.
For details, check out the [Theme Developers Guide](/docs/Theme%20Developers.md)
### 🛠 Bug Fixes & Quality-of-Life Improvements
Touchpad scrolling is now fully supported for easier navigation.

Thanks for your support and feedback—enjoy the update! 🎉
releases.name: The Sizable Update 1.4

releases.author: b-camphart
releases.description:
## What's Changed
- fixes blurriness on larger monitors
- adjusts the positioning of the controls
- fixes issue with incorrectly parsing date and datetime property types
- restores the ability for the plugin to detect properties that have an undefined type

## New Contributors
* VERY big thanks to @liamcain for their help https://github.com/b-camphart/timeline-view/pull/38

**Full Changelog**: https://github.com/b-camphart/timeline-view/compare/1.3.1...1.3.2
releases.name: The Edit Update 1.3.2

releases.author: b-camphart
releases.description:
Version **1.3.1** is a small update focused on refining the user experience and fixing a key bug related to group color updates. In this version, a few quality-of-life improvements were made, including:

- **Bug Fix:** Resolved an issue where group colors were not reliably updating.
- **Context Menu Options:** Added the ability to open timeline views directly from folder, tag, and search result context menus. This simplifies the process of creating focused timelines based on specific selections.
- **Re-Open Timeline Command:** A new command lets users re-open a timeline in its previous state, with an option to automatically open timelines with their last configuration.
- **Code Cleanup:** Minor code refactoring, including linting fixes and enhanced error handling.

This update is primarily focused on improving stability and usability.

**Full Changelog**: https://github.com/b-camphart/timeline-view/compare/1.3.0...1.3.1
releases.name: The Edit Update 1.3.1

releases.author: b-camphart
releases.description:
# 🚀 Introducing "The Edit Update" (v1.3.0)

This update brings a slew of powerful new features that make organizing your Obsidian vault easier and more intuitive than ever. From quick note creation to seamless multi-note editing, this update is all about giving you more control with less effort. Here's what’s new:

---

# 🌟 **New Features:**
### **✨ Double-Click to Create Notes:** 
Quickly create a new note with a double-click anywhere on the timeline. The new note automatically adopts the appropriate property value based on where you clicked, streamlining your workflow.
![create note](https://github.com/b-camphart/timeline-view/blob/main/docs/resources/create-note-example.gif)
  
### **📌 Drag & Drop to Edit Notes:** 
Effortlessly rearrange your notes by dragging and dropping them across the timeline. Adjust a single note's property or modify multiple notes at once with multi-selection support.
- Move a single note:
  ![move note](https://github.com/b-camphart/timeline-view/blob/main/docs/resources/move-single-note-example.gif)
- Move multiple notes at once:
  ![move note](https://github.com/b-camphart/timeline-view/blob/main/docs/resources/move-multiple-notes-example.gif)

### **Filter & Grouping Enhancements:**
Default ordering property, filter, and groups can now be set from plugin settings, making it easier to organize notes from the get-go.
![default settings](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/e2185f24-9b10-47ad-8b43-4f549c077d78)

---

### 🛠️ **Quality of Life Improvements:**
- **Timeline Context Menu:** New context options provide quicker access to common actions without the clutter.
  
- **Background Rows:** Improved visuals with background rows for better note separation and clarity.
  
- **Read-Only Toggle:** Switch between editable and read-only modes to prevent accidental changes.

- **Help Button:** Added a help button with a modal to provide in-app assistance.

---
releases.name: The Edit Update 1.3.0

releases.author: b-camphart
releases.description:
### Improvements
- Makes the design of hovering more consistent with canvas hovering behavior
- Makes the "where is this?" tooltip on the top of the timeline consistent with obsidian's tooltips
- (for theme developers) introduces new css variables for easily tweaking the design

### No Longer Broken
- Adjusts the position of the controls and settings to accommodate the new scrollbars
- Removed unused settings sections (to be re-introduced when features are completed)

**Full Changelog**: https://github.com/b-camphart/timeline-view/compare/1.2.1...1.2.2
releases.name: The Navigation Update 1.2.2

releases.author: b-camphart
releases.description:
### Improvements
- Can now open a linked markdown tab from within the timeline

### No Longer Broken
- filtering out, or deleting, a hovered item no longer causes the view to crash (#21)

**Full Changelog**: https://github.com/b-camphart/timeline-view/compare/1.2.0...1.2.1
releases.name: The Navigation Update 1.2.1

releases.author: b-camphart
releases.description:
This update focuses on adding navigational features, and resolving some missed design areas that should have been included in the initial release.

### Improvements
- Scrollbars so you can see where you're at in the timeline (both horizontal and vertical)
- Keyboard scroll support: Arrow Keys, Page Up, Page Down, Home, and End
- Navigate through items using the Tab key (Shift+Tab to navigate backward)
- Link the timeline with other tabs to keep focus on the currently open note

### No longer broken
- The top and bottom padding of the timeline "stage" area is now respected
- The hover tooltip now matches the tooltip used around all of Obsidian

**Full Changelog**: https://github.com/b-camphart/timeline-view/compare/1.1.1...1.2.0
releases.name: The Navigation Update
